text.title: Agent Observability Stack Blueprint Worked Example
text.summary: End-to-end worked example applying agent-observability-stack-blueprint to a representative case. Walks step-by-step through the procedure, shows the produced artefact, and highlights the rules it satisfies.

section.title: Setup
section.p: Context: a team that matches the Applies-If conditions for agent-observability-stack-blueprint. Inputs are populated per the Prerequisites table.

list.item: Step 1: Inventory current LLM calls, tool calls, retrieval calls; map to a span tree. — produced span map.
Step 2: Pick vendor by r5 decision tree. — produced vendor decision.
Step 3: Wire OTel SDK; emit spans for LLM/tool/retrieval. — produced OTel live.
Step 4: Build cost ledger schema and write-path. — produced ledger live.
Step 5: Wire LLM-judge sampler + drift dashboard with r3/r4 thresholds. — produced judge + dashboard.
Step 6: Document runbook: how to investigate a drift alert. — produced runbook.

section.title: Final artefact
section.p: The team commits the final artefact (per spec shape) at the version-controlled path declared in the output contract. Owner signs and links it to downstream consumers.

list.item: r1: Stack must emit OTel spans for every LLM call, tool call, and retrieval call.
r2: Each request has a cost ledger entry: provider, model, input tokens, output tokens, USD.
r3: LLM-judge runs on a sampled fraction of production traffic (1-10%) with a static rubric.
r4: Drift dashboard tracks p50/p95 of judge score over a 7-day rolling window; alert if 7-day drops by ≥0.1 vs baseline.
r5: Vendor pick follows the decision tree: self-host data + open-source budget → Langfuse; LangChain heavy → LangSmith; gate
